scope_boundary: Only the finite synthetic burst-review selector claim moves to synthetic-test-backed: the validator recomputes one 10-task local synthetic workload, compares admit-arrivals baseline against protected capacity smoothing, rejects a cheaper review-erasure negative control, checks residualized deferrals, and checks the finite Lean fixture bridge. This does not move the Resource Economics chapter core claim or any real load-stability, scheduler, TokenMana, PlanForge, human-productivity, economic, model-quality, safety, or deployment claim.
transition_reason: The local validation command checks a deterministic 10-task burst-review workload over three routes. The selected protected-capacity-smoothing route reduces recorded load-instability units from 5 under the admit-arrivals baseline to 0, preserves protected review, residualizes all 7 selected deferred task-ticks, and rejects the cheaper latency-only review-erasure route because it erases 3 protected reviews and hides 7 deferred task-ticks. The validator also checks the tracked public-safe result record and the finite ResourceEconomics Lean fixture theorem refs for workload validity, overload reduction, review-erasure rejection, residualized deferrals, and no support promotion. This is enough to accept the narrow finite selector claim, while preserving the existing no-change record for broader load-stability and chapter-core claims.
required_artifacts: Lean fixture alignment with the public result record
negative_results: route://negative-latency-only-review-erasure is rejected despite zero reported instability because it erases 3 protected high-risk reviews and hides 7 deferred task-ticks
the selected route records 7 deferred task-ticks as residualized costs rather than treating smoothing as free capacity

the existing no-change record for resource-economics.synthetic_load_stability_route_selection remains in force for broader real load-stability, deployed scheduler, TokenMana, PlanForge, reviewer-optimization, and economic claims
no Resource Economics chapter core claim is promoted above argument
no deployed scheduler, production queue, human-productivity, model-quality, benchmark, safety, source-interpretation, or economic outcome is claimed
downgrade_triggers: selected route no longer reduces finite baseline overload
selected route stops residualizing all deferred task-ticks
review-erasure negative control is accepted or loses protected-review violations
Lean fixture bridge drifts from the public result record
chapter or public prose describes this transition as real load stability, deployed scheduling, TokenMana, PlanForge, human-productivity, model-quality, safety, economic, or chapter-core evidence
promotion_burden: Broader Resource Economics, real load-stability, scheduler, or economic claims require a separate accepted transition with live or externally reviewed workload traces, production queue logs or public-safe replay packs, reviewer-capacity measurements, residual/displaced-cost accounting, hidden-cost audits, and independent review where relevant.
limitations: no production queue, deployed scheduler, TokenMana, or PlanForge runtime
no human-outcome, reviewer-capacity, serving-system, model-quality, benchmark, safety, or economic measurement
does not promote any chapter core claim above argument
reviewer_independence: local maintainer-agent review only; not an independent scheduler, queueing, economics, workload-quality, human-outcome, or external safety audit
non_claims: does not promote any chapter core claim

does not prove real load stability, deployed scheduling, TokenMana, PlanForge, reviewer-optimization, human productivity, economic behavior, model quality, benchmark performance, safety, or ASI capability
does not prove source interpretation, simulator adequacy, production queue behavior, serving-system behavior, or workload-quality improvement outside the finite synthetic workload
does not replace future live traces, public replay packs, external review, production logs, hidden-cost audits, or independent review
